In an interview for Al Mayadeen, Professor Marandi said he believes the chances of "conflict are very high,” noting that the United States did not deploy additional troops to the region after the 39-day Ramadan war merely to “sit there and do nothing.”

Addressing the messages exchanged between Iran and the United States, Marandi pointed out that such communication is not new, as it has been ongoing for decades, but has intensified in recent months and weeks due to the war.

He added that while Iran may present new proposals, it will not allow its sovereignty to be undermined. Professor Marandi explained that unless the United States comes to terms with the reality on several issues, including Iran’s peaceful nuclear program and its control of the Strait of Hormuz, "siege warfare" will continue, and "military conflict" is likely in the coming days or weeks.

@s_m_marandi 

#AlMayadeen #StraitofHormuz #IranWar #Trump

https://t.co/MLAHQqTgue

In a post on X, Mohsen Rezaei, a member of Iran’s Expediency Discernment Council, took a jab at US leadership and attempted justifications for the war on Iran.

Rezaei, describing US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth as "inexperienced and ignorant", argued that had he had a proper understanding of Iran’s geography and the strategic complexity of the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf would have prevented the US from dragging its forces into "a quagmire where it now has neither a way forward nor a way back."

He warned that the United States "will learn many lessons in the Persian Gulf and the Sea of Oman."

Head of #Iran’s judiciary, Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei, asserted that Iran does not seek war but "does not fear it", but "If our dignity is threatened, we will fight for it."

He said the country has "never left the negotiating table" and has "always welcomed negotiations based on logic and rationality," but added clearly that "we most certainly do not accept diktats."

Ejei noted that this position is shared across the system in a unified stance, arguing that "the enemy, which has achieved none of its goals through aggression and threats, will not be able to impose its dictates and ambitions at the negotiating table."

He also confirmed that Iran will continue "legal and judicial follow-up on #US-Israeli aggression and  war crimes," vowing to pursue accountability and reparations "both domestically and internationally with full force."

Praising public sentiment and unity, he pointed to the "'Soul devoted to Iran' campaign, which gathered more than 30 million volunteers," saying it "struck fear into the hearts of enemies."

He further affirmed that Iran’s "diplomacy is an extension of the battlefield, under the orders of the Leader of the Revolution," while warning that a "defeated and scarred enemy will not stand idly by and will expand the scope of its conspiracies day by day; the possibility of renewed aggression remains."

#IranWar #WarOnIran

📌أسهم الحرب مرتفعة لا سيما مع الانتشار الكثيف للقوات الأميركية في المنطقة

📌إيران ثابتة على موقفها بشأن مضيق هرمز وبرنامجها النووي السلمي وحماية سيادتها

الخبير بالشؤون الاستراتيجية، محمد مرندي، لـ #الميادين
